adds.rationale: Welltower enters to fill the demographics slot, which has zero representation in the current watchlist; Q2 2025 earnings (July 28) showed +21.9% FFO growth, a 4% revenue beat, and +23.4% Seniors Housing Operating NOI, while $9.2B in year-to-date acquisitions signals an active buildup phase against a structural US 80+ population growth tailwind and a supply pipeline constrained to 2% or less per year.

news_evidence.summary: Welltower Q2 2025 results: normalized FFO $1.28/share (+21.9% YoY); total portfolio same-store NOI +13.8%; Seniors Housing Operating same-store NOI +23.4%; $9.2 billion in year-to-date transaction volume; full-year 2025 FFO guidance raised.

Welltower Q2 2025 earnings call transcript: revenue $2.55B vs $2.45B expected (4.08% beat); Seniors Housing Operating revenue +10.1% driven by 420 bps occupancy growth and Revenue Per Occupied Room +4.9%; WELL stock rose 3.47% to $160.52 on the day.

US population aged 80 and above projected to grow 28% in the next five years while senior housing supply additions are expected to remain at 2% or less per year, creating a durable structural supply-demand imbalance that underpins healthcare REIT NOI growth.

removes.rationale: Removing MSFT to trim AI-bucket concentration from three names to two: FY25 Q4 results showed cloud gross margin compressed to 69% as AI infrastructure capital spend scales, AI-service gross margins running approximately 60% versus traditional software's 80%, and capex guided above $40B for the next quarter — the stock declined on strong headline numbers, a classic peak-enthusiasm signal — and with GOOGL covering search/AI platforms and NVDA covering AI infrastructure hardware, MSFT provides the weakest incremental differentiation of the three AI names.

news_evidence.summary: Microsoft FY25 Q4 (quarter ended June 30, 2025): Cloud gross margin percentage decreased to 69%, driven by the impact of scaling AI infrastructure; CapEx expected to increase to over $40B in Q1 FY26 as capacity comes online, including approximately $5B from higher component pricing.

MSFT Q4 deep dive: AI services gross margin approximately 60% versus approximately 80% for traditional software; CFO Amy Hood acknowledged data center depreciation would compress gross margins for several more quarters; analysts questioned the timeline to recover returns on massive AI capex.

Hyperscaler enterprise AI adoption broad (80-90% of firms) but shallow (fewer than 40% have scaled beyond pilot programs), widening the gap between hyperscaler capex commitments and near-term monetization; bond spreads increased and median equity prices declined even as Q2 2025 results printed, signaling investor skepticism on capex payback timelines.

rationale_overall: This rebalance makes one swap — MSFT out, WELL in — that serves two simultaneous goals from the investor profile. First, it reduces AI-bucket concentration: holding GOOGL, MSFT, and NVDA in an eight-name watchlist dedicates 37.5% of watchlist slots to one wave; with FY25 Q4 signaling margin compression ahead for the AI infrastructure buildout and the market already pricing perfection into the megacaps, removing the most redundant AI name (MSFT) before the crest is consistent with operating rule one. GOOGL and NVDA stay because each holds a distinct role: GOOGL has the antitrust-relief catalyst and search-AI monetization, NVDA has the Blackwell hardware ramp and the purest AI infrastructure moat. Second, it fills the demographics slot, which the profile explicitly names as a watched non-technology wave but the portfolio has never held; Welltower's Q2 2025 results show the wave is already in active buildup — $9.2B in year-to-date acquisitions, +21.9% FFO growth, a 4% revenue beat — while the US 80-plus population growth and constrained housing supply provide the structural tailwind. All other holdings are kept: RKLB posted record Q2 2025 revenue of $144.5M (+36% YoY); IONQ completed the Oxford Ionics acquisition on September 17 and achieved AQ-64 ahead of schedule; NVDA guided Q3 FY26 to $54B; NUKZ benefits from the ongoing AI data center power-purchase-agreement thesis; BOTZ and ITA remain well-supported by the robotics and defense-rearmament waves respectively.
